prerequisites: A PayPal account: a PayPal Developer account is sufficient for testing US integrations, while going live or testing outside the US requires a PayPal Business account.
A REST app created in the PayPal Developer Dashboard (under Apps & Credentials) to obtain a client ID and client secret.

A decision on environment: sandbox (test) versus live (production), set via the required is_sandbox config field. Sandbox mirrors real-world transactions for testing; live processes real money and requires completing the go-live process.
A start_date value (required) marking the earliest point from which to pull transaction data.

authMethods.name: OAuth 2.0 client credentials (client_id + client_secret)
authMethods.summary: PayPal REST APIs authenticate with OAuth 2.0 access tokens. The connector exchanges the client_id and client_secret (via HTTP Basic auth, grant_type=client_credentials) at the PayPal token endpoint to obtain a short-lived bearer access token, which is then sent as Authorization: Bearer on each API request. The client_id identifies the application and the client_secret authenticates it; keep the secret confidential. Both are issued per environment, so use sandbox credentials when is_sandbox is enabled and live credentials otherwise.

authMethods.name: OAuth refresh token (optional)
authMethods.summary: The optional refresh_token secret field corresponds to PayPal's OAuth flow. It is not required for the standard client-credentials grant, so leave it blank unless your integration was issued a refresh token. When supplied, it is stored as a secret alongside client_id and client_secret.

setupSteps.title: Create or sign in to a PayPal account
setupSteps.body: Sign in to the PayPal Developer Dashboard. Use a PayPal Developer account for testing US integrations; for live use or testing outside the US, use a PayPal Business account. Complete the go-live process before using live (production) credentials.

setupSteps.title: Create a REST app and copy credentials
setupSteps.body: In the Developer Dashboard, go to Apps & Credentials. Use the default app provided with new accounts or select Create App. Copy the generated client ID and client secret. Note that credentials differ between the Sandbox and Live tabs, so copy them from the tab matching the environment you will use.

setupSteps.title: Choose the environment
setupSteps.body: Set the required is_sandbox field to control which PayPal environment the connector targets. Enable it to use the sandbox (test) endpoints with sandbox credentials; disable it to use live (production) endpoints with live credentials. The client_id and client_secret you provide must come from the matching environment.

setupSteps.title: Provide credentials in the connector
setupSteps.body: Enter the client_id and client_secret in the connector's secret fields. Optionally provide a refresh_token only if your integration was issued one; the standard client-credentials flow does not require it.

setupSteps.title: Set the sync window
setupSteps.body: Set start_date (required) as the earliest date to pull transaction data. Optionally set end_date to bound the range, dispute_start_date to control the start date for dispute data, and time_window to tune how the connector slices the requested date range into API calls.
